# Skill: Discover MCP

> Detect available MCP servers and generate configuration so other skills know which tools to use.
>
> **When**: First setup (`make init`), on demand (`make discover-mcp`), or automatically when a skill needs an MCP tool and doesn't know which is available.
> **Output**: `.github/generated/mcp-instructions.md`

---

## Step 1: Probe Available Tools

Detection patterns are in copilot-instructions.md Â§ MCP Tools. Search each prefix pattern â€” if results found, that server is available.

---

## Step 2: Map Capabilities

| Domain | MCP Priority | Best Tools | Fallback |
|--------|-------------|------------|----------|
| **Ticketing (Jira)** | Atlassian | `mcp_atlassian_searchJiraIssuesUsingJql`, `mcp_atlassian_getJiraIssue` | Ask for ticket key |
| **Ticketing (GitHub)** | GitHub | `mcp_github_search_issues`, `mcp_github_issue_read` | Ask for ticket key |
| **Git ops** | terminal | `git status`, `git branch`, `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` | â€” |
| **Pull Requests** | GitHub | `mcp_github_create_pull_request`, `mcp_github_pull_request_read` | Manual PR creation |
| **Browser/UI** | Playwright | `mcp_microsoft_pla_browser_navigate`, `browser_take_screenshot` | Skip or manual check |
| **Code Intel** | Pylance | `mcp_pylance_*` | grep/read_file |
| **Web Search** | Tavily / Context7 | `mcp_io_github_tav_tavily_search`, `mcp_io_github_ups_*` | Codebase-only analysis |

---

## Step 3: Generate MCP Instructions File

Create `.github/generated/mcp-instructions.md`:

```markdown
# MCP Configuration (Auto-Generated)

> Generated by discover-mcp skill on <date>.
> Re-run: ask "discover MCP tools" or `make discover-mcp`
> Do not edit manually â€” changes will be overwritten.

## Detected Servers

| Server | Status | Capabilities |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Atlassian | âś… Available | Jira JQL, Confluence |
| GitHub | âś… Available | PRs, code search, issues, reviews |
| Playwright | âś… Available | Browser automation, screenshots |
| Tavily | âťŚ Not detected | â€” |
| Context7 | âťŚ Not detected | â€” |

## Tool Mapping

List the best tool per action for each detected server. Example structure:

```
### Ticketing
- Search: `mcp_atlassian_searchJiraIssuesUsingJql`
- Details: `mcp_atlassian_getJiraIssue`

### PRs
- Create: `mcp_github_create_pull_request`
- Read: `mcp_github_pull_request_read`

### Git
- Status/Branch/Commit/Push: `git` CLI

### Browser
- Navigate/Screenshot/Click: `mcp_microsoft_pla_browser_*`
```

## Graceful Degradation

| Missing | Fallback |
|---------|----------|
| Ticketing MCP | Ask for ticket key directly |
| GitHub MCP | Present PR body as markdown for manual creation |
| Git MCP | Terminal `git` commands |
| Playwright MCP | Skip UI verification, note "manual check required" |
| Web search MCP | Codebase-only analysis |
```

---

## Step 4: Present Summary

Report: N servers detected, M not found, list enabled/missing capabilities.

File generated at `.github/generated/mcp-instructions.md`.

---

## Re-Running

Delete old file â†’ re-probe all patterns â†’ generate fresh â†’ note what changed.

Skills check tools at runtime. The generated file is a reference, not a gate â€” always handle MCP tool failures gracefully.
